rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the framing, contribution claim, and conceptual plausibility of TransCollab.
rationale.R1.2: A general reaction to the draft centrally includes checking whether the mechanism, ablations, and conclusions form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The excerpt makes empirical claims, so judging whether the reported results support the stated conclusions is a central responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of an open-ended opinion request must preserve uncertainty about the intended depth and evaluation criteria.
rationale.RX.2: The delegation requires guarding against endorsing claims such as generalization or gap-closing beyond what the excerpt establishes.
rationale.RX.3: The response should remain within assessment of the provided research-writing artifact rather than performing edits or external validation.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid encouraging misleading scientific claims or unsupported downstream use of the framework.
rationale.RX.5: The assessment should remain traceable to specific claims and evidence contained in the provided artifact.
